Name: 2024 Early College Data Story
Description: This data story highlights insights from the 2024 Early College Data Dashboard on student participation and college credits earned by semester, school, and partnership. It describes the program and goals and features two Early College Designated Partnerships, in the Worcester Public Schools and New Heights Charter School of Brockton.

Name: ACCESS for ELLs Composite Proficiency Level
Description: This dataset contains the number and percent of students at each proficiency level in four composite areas of the ACCESS for ELLs test (Oral Language, Literacy, Comprehension, Overall) from 2022 onward. Massachusetts administers the ACCESS for ELLs test annually to all English learners in publicly funded K-12 schools, as required by state and federal law. The test evaluates students in the four subdomains of speaking, listening, reading, and writing. In addition to scores for each subdomain, results are reported in composite scores for comprehension, oral language, literacy, and overall English proficiency. Composite scores are created from two or more domain scores in listening, speaking, reading, and writing. Only students who complete all four domains of the standard test are included in composite scores. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: ACCESS for ELLs Composite Proficiency Level Report

Name: ACCESS for ELLs Reporting Elements
Description:
This dataset contains the number and percent of students in ACCESS for ELLs reporting elements 1, 2, 3, and 5 of Title III ESSA requirements from the year 2022 onward. Massachusetts administers the ACCESS for ELLs test annually to all English learners in publicly funded K-12 schools, as required by state and federal law. There are four elements included in this dataset:
  • Reporting Element 1: The number and percent of ELs who make progress towards English language proficiency
  • Reporting Element 2: The number and percent of ELs who attain an ACCESS score indicating English language proficiency
  • Reporting Element 3: The number and percentage of ELs who exit English learner classification based on their attainment of English language proficiency
  • Reporting Element 5: The number and percentage of ELs who have not attained English proficiency within six years of initial classification as an English learner
Reporting Element 4 (the number and percentage of former ELs who have met challenging state academic standards for each of the four years after exiting EL status) is not included in this dataset. For more information about the test administration and reporting elements, please see the WIDA ACCESS page on the Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's website. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: ACCESS for ELLs Reporting Elements

Name: ACCESS for ELLs Results: Resource Page
Description:
This page hosts downloadable files with ACCESS for ELLs test results by district and school. ACCESS for ELLs scores provide information on students' English proficiency. They do not measure students' academic achievement or content knowledge.  

Federal and state laws require that English learner (EL) students be assessed annually to measure their proficiency in reading, writing, listening, and speaking English, as well as the progress they are making in learning English. In fulfillment of these laws, EL students are required to participate in ACCESS for ELLs tests. ACCESS for ELLs tests are administered annually in January-February.

Name: Advanced Course Completion
Description: This report shows the number and rate of Massachusetts public school students in grades 11 and 12 who completed advanced coursework since 2018. Advanced coursework includes the following: Advanced Placement, International Baccalaureate, Project Lead the Way, dual enrollment courses, Chapter 74-approved vocational/technical secondary cooperative education programs, and other selected rigorous courses. For a full list of advanced coursework, please see the List of Advanced Courses for Accountability Reporting on the Accountability Lists, Materials, and Tools website. Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Advanced Course Completion Report

Name: Arts Course Taking
Description: This dataset contains the number and percentage of Massachusetts public school students completing at least one arts course in grades K-12 since 2018. Arts courses are reported in the Student Course Schedule (SCS) system and align with the Massachusetts Arts Curriculum Framework. For a full list of courses and subjects, see the Arts Course List. Beginning with the 2019-2020 school year, the arts course list was updated the "Media Arts" category. In addition, a number of arts courses were reclassified from the "General Arts" category to "Visual Arts" to more closely align to the industry standard. Since 2022, courses are no longer assigned to the "General" category. Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Arts Coursetaking Report

Description:
See notice below about this dataset This dataset provides the average annual earnings by industry per district. Wage records are obtained from the Massachusetts Department of Unemployment Assistance (DUA) using a secure, anonymized matching process with limitations. For details on the process and suppression rules, please visit the Employment and Earnings of High School Graduates dashboard. This dataset is one of three containing the same data that is also published in the Employment and Earnings of High School Graduates dashboard: Average Earnings by Student Group Average Earnings by Industry College and Career Outcomes List of Industries

2025 Update on DESE Data on Employment and Earnings  The data link between high school graduates and future earnings makes it possible to follow students beyond high school and college into the workforce, enabling long-term evaluation of educational programs using workforce outcomes. While DESE has published these data in the past, as of June 2025 we are temporarily pausing updates due to an issue conducting the link that was brought to our attention in 2023 by a team of researchers. The issue impacts the earnings information for students who never attended a postsecondary institution or who only attended private or out-of-state colleges or universities, beginning with the 2017 high school graduation cohort, with growing impact in each successive high school graduation cohort. The issue does not impact the earnings information for students who attended a Massachusetts public institution of higher education, and earnings data for those students will continue to be updated. Once a solution is found, the past cohorts of data with low match rates will be updated. DESE and partner agencies are exploring linking strategies to maximize the utility of the information. More detailed information can be found in the attached memo provided by the research team from the Annenberg Institute. We thank them for calling this issue to our attention.

Name: Awards Conferred Meta-Major to Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) Codes
Description: This dataset provides a crosswalk that maps the meta-major column to the Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) classification columns in the Awards (Degrees) Conferred Statewide dataset. Meta-majors are a framework that categorizes academic programs into broad curricular pathways. This dataset utilizes the 2020 CIP. The Classification of Instructional Programs was created by the U.S. Department of Education's National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) in 1980. More information can be found on the CIP user site. This crosswalk is unique to this dataset and should not be used to map award information from other sources or institutions.

Name: Career Technical Education (CTE) Data
Description:
This page provides data and reporting resources for school districts and public two-year colleges offering Career Technical Education (CTE) programs overseen by the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education. Laws and regulations require districts, public two-year colleges, and the state to assess the effectiveness of CTE programs through the collection of student-level performance data, and to disaggregate it for populations of students and by program. These can be used to make progress toward improving student performance. Available data include:
  • CTE Enrollment Dashboard (Power BI)
  • CTE Perkins IV Core Indicators Dashboard (Power BI)
  • CTE Perkins V Core Indicators Dashboard (Power BI)
  • Graduate Follow-up Survey Results (XLSX)

Name: Chapter 70 Foundation Budget and Net School Spending (NSS)
Description:
This dataset contains Chapter 70 foundation budget and net school spending data from 2008 onward. The Chapter 70 program is the major program of state aid to public elementary and secondary schools providing state aid to support school operations. It establishes minimum spending requirements for each school district and minimum requirements for each municipality's share of school costs. Detailed numbers for the Chapter 70 formula are available on the School Finance area of the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education’s website. Key files on this site include:
  • Chapter 70 District Profiles – data including key formula components including foundation enrollment, foundation budget, Chapter 70 aid and required net school spending from 2008 to the most recent year of data available
  • Chapter 70 Trends in Aid and Local Contribution – graphs and data at the district level comparing key formula components including foundation enrollment, foundation budget, Chapter 70 aid, and required net school spending from 2007 to the most recent year of data available
  • Compliance with Net School Spending Requirements – data on net school spending at the district level from 2017 to the most recent year of data available
This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Chapter 70 Foundation Budget and Net School Spending
Tags: state aid,nss,spending,public school,net school spending,foundation budget,district,costs,chapter 70,c70

Name: Chapter 70 Program Information and Data
Description:
This page hosts resources to help users understand state aid to Massachusetts public schools. It contains information about legislation and regulations, spending requirements, analytic reports, and other features. It also includes two downloadable Excel workbooks:
  • Chapter 70 District Profiles - Shows yearly spending and state aid totals since 1993 by district in comparison to the foundation budget.
    • Chapter 70 Trends in Aid and Local Contribution - Shows trend data for the key factors influencing Chapter 70 required local contributions and state aid calculations since 2007.
The Chapter 70 program is the major program of state aid to public elementary and secondary schools. In addition to providing state aid to support school operations, it also establishes minimum spending requirements for each school district and minimum requirements for each municipality's share of school costs.

Name: Charter Analysis and Review Tool (CHART): Resource Page
Description:
CHART is designed to assist charter schools in monitoring their progress with their Recruitment and Retention Plans which includes deliberate, specific strategies the school will use to attract, to enroll, and to retain a student population that is demographically comparable to similar grades in schools from which the charter school enrolls students. It is a pair of Excel workbooks that provide multi-school, multi-year demographic comparison data in the following categories:
CHART — Enrollment Enrollment percentages by selected populations and race. CHART — Indicators Attrition percentages by grade level and selected populations/race. Student indicator statistics, including suspensions by selected population/race.
The charter schools are graphed against comparable schools in its region, the median and first quartile of the comparison schools, and the state average. For enrollment demographic indicators, where possible, a statistically-derived "Comparison Index" is also graphed to provide a comparison figure that account for the charter school's size and fluctuations in student populations that yield natural variation in school-level enrollment figures, along with a "Gap Narrowing Target", which provides reference point for progress over time.

Name: Charter School Dashboard
Description: This dashboard provides detailed data about charter schools in Massachusetts. It contains a variety of indicators across many categories, including enrollment, academics, attendance, suspension, retention, attrition, mobility, graduation, and dropout. Indicators are disaggregated by grade and student group, and are contextualized with comparison data. For more information about the Charter School Dashboard, visit the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's website on Board Governance and Charter Amendments.

Name: Children Enrolled in Formal Early Education and Care Statewide
Description: This dataset includes the estimated number of children statewide and by region using data from the US Census, as well as the subset of children estimated to be enrolled in formal early education and care programs across the mixed delivery system. Additionally, the dataset includes the total number of children in families with household income below 50% of the state median income and whether all adults in the household are in the labor force, based on data from the US Census, along with the number of children who receive Child Care Financial Assistance (CCFA). This dataset comes from the Child Universal Success Platform (CUSP), an integrated data system developed by Third Sector Intelligence (3Si) in partnership with the Department of Early Education and Care (EEC). CUSP models the full child population in the Commonwealth and estimates the extent to which families are participating in the formal early education and care system. CUSP creates a child population base, drawing on data from the US Census, and uses aggregate capacity data from EEC's Licensing Education Analytic Database (LEAD), aggregate enrollment data from programs participating in Commonwealth Cares for Children (C3), as well as aggregate pre-K enrollment data for district public schools and private schools to estimate the proportion of children served by formal early education and care programs across the mixed delivery system. In this dataset, "formal early education and care" includes enrollment in EEC licensed and funded center based care, family child care programs, district public preschools, and private pre-K programs, but does not include public/private K-12 enrollment or district run afterschool programs. This dataset is part of EEC's Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Dashboard.

Name: Class Size by Gender, Race/Ethnicity, and Selected Populations
Description: This dataset provides data about classes by subject in Massachusetts public schools and districts since 2011. It includes the number of classes in a given subject, the average class size, and demographic indicators of students taking those classes. This data allows for analyzing trends in class size distribution across different subjects and student subgroups. It can support research on educational equity, resource allocation within schools, and potential disparities in learning environments. Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Class Size by Race/Ethnicity Class Size by Gender and Selected Population

Name: Collaborative Level of Need
Description: This dataset details educational collaboratives that serve students with disabilities, categorized by the intensity of their needs between 2013 and 2016. Educational collaboratives are formed through an agreement among two or more school committees to provide educational programs or services for their member school systems. The educational services provided vary from program to program, but in general, collaboratives offer special education services that would be difficult to provide locally. Many collaboratives also offer vocational education programs and professional development programs. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Collaborative Level of Need

Name: College and Career Outcomes Report
Description: This report presents data and information about student preparedness for workforce and post-graduate success in Massachusetts, including high school enrollment, graduation, coursework, participation in Designated High School College and Career Pathways (After Dark, Career Connections, Career Technical Education, Early College, and Innovation Career Pathways), college attendance and persistence, and wages.

Name: Commonwealth Cares for Children (C3) Funds by Program
Description: C3 provides monthly operational funding to child care programs across Massachusetts. Each row in this dataset represents the amount of C3 funds disbursed to a program by fiscal year. The state's fiscal year begins on July 1 and ends on June 30. The C3 funds totals do not include the FY22 Workforce Bonus or FY23 Subsidized Provider Bonus. The data may not reflect all adjustments made to payment amounts. Individual family child care program names are excluded from the data.

Name: Connecting to data tools
Description: Work with datasets in your preferred platform for analysis and visualization by downloading and importing data or connecting via API or OData.

Name: Current Licensed and Funded Early Education and Care Programs
Description:
This dataset includes a snapshot of programs currently licensed or funded by the Department of Early Education and Care (EEC). The data is pulled from EEC's Licensing Education Analytic Database (LEAD). For a list of all current and historical early education and care programs please visit the Licensed and Funded Early Education and Care Programs dataset. Data are updated monthly at the beginning of each month.

Licensed programs are programs that are subject to licensing regulations by EEC, which include meeting health and safety standards, training and professional development requirements, criminal and background checks, and maintaining appropriate ratios of educators to children. Licensed programs can include any of the following: family child care and group and school age programs. For more information on licensed programs click here.

Funded programs are programs that are not subject to licensure by EEC but have the approval from EEC to provide subsidized child care for low-income or at-risk families. Funded programs can include any of the following: public schools; private schools; religious schools; and government, tribal, and military child care programs. For more information on funded providers click here.

The types of programs in this dataset include:

  • Licensed Family Child Care is child care provided in someone's home where the caretaker is not related to the children.

  • Licensed Center-Based Care, sometimes called group and school age programs, provide early education and care for young children in a non-residential setting such as a school or community center. These programs include several types of early education and care such as: Center-based care for infants, toddlers, preschool, or kindergarten age children; Early Head Start and Head Start programs; and Out-of- school time programs that operate before/after school or during the summer.

  • Funded Public Schools that participate in CCFA are included here and public schools that do not participate in CCFA are not included here. For data on all public schools with pre-K enrollment, please see the Pre-Kindergarten and Kindergarten Enrollment dataset.

  • Funded Private Schools that participate in CCFA are included here and private schools that do not participate in CCFA are not included here. For data on all private schools with pre-K enrollment, please see the Non-Public School Enrollment dataset.

Name: DARTs English Learners: Dashboard
Description: The DART for English Learners (ELs) dashboard consolidate state data on English learners into a single tool. This tool can be used to understand EL students’ demographics, progress, and outcomes as well as to compare districts with each other and the state. The information contained in this report is closely tied with the Office of Language Acquisition's program review requirements. The DARTs provide a gauge of the overall condition of a district or school, but do not have all available information. They should be treated as a good starting point for exploring the data and identifying areas of focus for further inquiry. Please see the Info tab on the dashboard for detailed data analysis considerations.

Name: DARTs: Resource Page
Description: The District Analysis and Review Tools (DARTs) turn the Department's vast amount of data into valuable, easily consumable information. The DARTs offer snapshots of district and school performance, allowing users to easily track select data elements over time, and make sound, meaningful comparisons to the state or to "comparable" organizations. The data elements in each DART tool are linked to a broader strategic framework defining the characteristics of effective educational organizations and cover a broad range of district and school interests including demographic, assessment, student support, educator, financial, and achievement gap data. The DARTs, in combination with other tools found on this website, may be utilized by district and school leaders in enacting each of the six district standards upon which district reviews, improvement planning, and other forms of accountability and assistance are based: Leadership & Governance, Curriculum & Instruction, Assessment, Human Resources & Professional Development, Student Support, and Financial & Asset Management.

Name: DARTs Success After High School: Dashboard
Description:
The DART: Success After High School Dashboard is a tool used to support the self-evaluation process for whole districts as well as individual schools. It contains a set of data elements  provide an indication of the overall condition of a district or school's efforts to ensure all students are ready for their next steps as productive and contributing members of society.

This tool contains data elements that cover a range of school, district, post-secondary and career readiness information including demographics, high school indicators, high school performance, programs of study, post-secondary education outcomes, and career development education.

The DARTs provide a gauge of the overall condition of a district or school, but do not have all available information. They should be treated as a good starting point for exploring the data and identifying areas of focus for further inquiry. Please see the Info tab on the dashboard for detailed data analysis considerations.

Function codes describe discrete expenditures within major functional areas such as administration, teaching, and maintenance. The report also distinguishes between in-district and out-of-district programs and spending is categorized by funding source, including general fund and grants and revolving funds. For more information about the data and how to interpret it, please visit the School Finance Dashboard. This dataset only includes school district expenditures. Due to differences how data is reported by school districts and charter schools and virtual schools, charter school and virtual school data is not available at the function code level. Visit the charter school financial report page for more information. Name: Dropout Report
Description: This dataset provides the number and percentage Massachusetts public high school students who dropped out of high school since 2008. It also includes the percentage of dropouts by grade. Dropout rate is calculated as the percentage of students in a given grade who dropped out of school between July 1 and June 30 prior to the listed year and who did not return to school by the following October 1. Dropouts are defined as students who leave school prior to graduation for reasons other than transfer to another school. Dropout rates are not reported for any student group where the number of students is less than 6. Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Dropout Report

Name: Early Childhood Educator Workforce
Description: This dataset includes the estimated number of early childhood educators in the workforce across Massachusetts. Early childhood educator roles are available by licensing region and program type. Workforce data is collected bi-annually from the Commonwealth Cares for Children (C3) application. Note that the number of family child care licensees reflects the number of licensed family child care programs in the Licensed and Funded Child Care Providers dataset. This data set is part of EEC's Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Dashboard.

Name: Early Childhood Integrated Data System (ECIDS)

Name: Early College creates pathways to higher education
Early College creates pathways to higher education
Description: This revised data story explores trends in participation and outcomes based on insights from the Early College Data Dashboard.

Name: Early College Credits
Description: This dataset contains data on credits registered and earned by students in designated Early College programs since school year 2021-22. Early College is a program that designates partnerships between high schools and colleges to support high school students to complete college courses. The list of designated partnerships is available here. Students are counted in this dataset if they are marked as an Early College student by the district. Credits are counted in this dataset if they are submitted to DHE. The credits are counted based on where they are taken, even if that is an institution of higher ed (IHE) outside of the student's designated Early College partnership. Credits from Fall and Spring semester are counted; summer credits are not counted. Data includes any credits at public IHEs (Wentworth Institute of Technology data have not been submitted as of July 2024), and '22-23 data from private IHEs that are part of an Early College designated partnership. The dataset is updated in the fall of each year to add in the previous year's credits. Credit counts are suppressed (hidden) for groups in which there are fewer than 6 students to protect student privacy, though those credits are still counted in totals. The data here are the same as the credit data in the MA Early College Dashboard.

Name: Early College Participation
Description: This dataset contains data on the number of students participating in designated Early College programs since school year 2020-21. Early College is a program that designates partnerships between high schools and colleges to support high school students to complete college courses. The list of designated partnerships is available here. Students are counted in this dataset if they are marked as an Early College student by the district. The district also submits each student's affiliation with a single institution of higher education (IHE), though some Early College students take credits at more than one IHE. The period column allows you to filter for Fall or Spring, or to see the full-year deduplicated count of participants. The dataset is updated after each semester, when the SIMS collection for that semester is certified. The data here are the same as the participation data in the MA Early College Dashboard. Data note: For the Fall 2021 collection, only 2 digits of the college code were stored, so college names could not be loaded. The incomplete 2-digit codes are shown in this dataset, but the college name field is blank for that collection.

Name: Early Education and Care's Centralized Waitlist for Child Care Financial Assistance (CCFA)
Description: This dataset includes the number of children who are on EEC's centralized waitlist for Child Care Financial Assistance (CCFA). Families may be added to the waitlist by any Family Access Administrator which includes, but is not limited to, Child Care Resource and Referral Agency (CCR&R), Contracted Child Care Provider, or Mass 211. The data is both longitudinal and disaggregated by Subsidy Region. This dataset is part of EEC's Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Dashboard.

Name: Educator Data: Dashboard
Description: This dashboard displays state and district level educator data by race and ethnicity. It was created to support the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's commitment to provide all students with a racially diverse and culturally responsive educator workforce. The Employed Educators Report provides the most recent three years of data for educators employed in Massachusetts public schools. Total educators, new hires, retention, experienced educators, and in-field data are displayed by job classification. This dashboard contains the same data that is also published in the following datasets in the E2C Hub: Educator Dashboard: MTEL Educator Dashboard: Licensure Educator Dashboard: Educator Preparation Educator Dashboard: Teacher Indicators Educator Dashboard: Total Educators, Retention, and New Hires

Name: Educator Licensure by Race/Ethnicity
Description:
This dataset displays educator licensure data by race and ethnicity with rows for different years. It was created as part of a dashboard supporting the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's commitment to provide all students with a racially diverse and culturally responsive educator workforce.
Educator Licensure: This displays the number and percent of individuals who were issued an emergency, provisional, and initial license within the school year (September 1 to August 31).
This dataset contains the same data that is also published in the Educator Licensure table on our Educator Dashboard.

Name: Educator Preparation Candidate Enrollment
Description: This dataset shows the number of candidates who enrolled in an approved Massachusetts educator preparation program by race/ethnicity and gender since 2012. The "Total Count" column represents the unduplicated number of candidates enrolled in each educator preparation program. Information is reported by the educator preparation program provider for the academic year (September 1st – August 31st). This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Ed Prep Candidate Enrollment
Tags: educator preparation program,enrollment,educator pipeline,demographics,race and ethnicity,gender

Name: Educator Preparation Candidate Enrollment and Program Completion by Race/Ethnicity
Description:
This dataset displays educator preparation data by race and ethnicity with rows for different years. It was created as part of a dashboard supporting the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's commitment to provide all students with a racially diverse and culturally responsive educator workforce.
Educator Preparation: This displays the number and percent of candidates who enrolled and completed their program in the program year. This is based on the individuals' furthest, or most recently reported, point in the selected program year as reported by the organization.
This dataset contains the same data that is also published in the Educator Preparation table on our Educator Dashboard.

Name: Educator Preparation Employment by Program
Description: This dataset provides employment information about candidates who completed an approved Massachusetts educator preparation program since 2013. It includes counts and percentages of educators who were employed in a Massachusetts public school or district by June of a given school year, counts of educators who were retained, and percentages of educators who remained employed for two years. Employment rates are reported by program completion year, program subject area, and license level. Employment is determined by matching program completers reported by preparation program providers with employment data collected from districts in the Education Personnel Information Management System (EPIMS). The number in the retention cohort includes the number of program completers first employed at least one year prior to the report year, and is the numerator for the retention rates. Note: Data only includes program completers who became employed in a Massachusetts public, K-12 school, including charter schools, and collaboratives. The data do not include employment in private schools or outside of Massachusetts. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Ed Prep Employment by Program
Tags: educators,ed prep,teachers,employment,educator pipeline,educator preparation,educator preparation program

Name: Educator Preparation Program Completers
Description: This dataset shows the number of candidates who completed an approved Massachusetts educator preparation program by institution, license level, and the type of program completed since 2011. It includes the total number of candidates meeting all requirements of the preparation program (i.e., instruction/course work and practicum), regardless of whether a candidate has taken and passed state tests or assessments for licensure or has been endorsed for licensure by the program. Note: "All Program Completers" may double count candidates who completed two programs during the same year. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Ed Prep Program Completers

Name: Educator Preparation Program Staff by Race/Ethnicity and Gender
Description: This dataset provides information regarding the educator preparation program faculty and staff by race/ethnicity and gender from 2012 onward. It includes the full-time equivalent (FTE) count of educator preparation program faculty and staff, as well as the number of students per faculty member in those programs. Faculty and staff may be reported in one or more racial groups. Blanks indicate that no information was reported. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Educator Preparation Faculty by Race/Gender

Name: Edwin Analytics: Resource Page
Description: Edwin Analytics is a secure reporting and data analysis tool that gives authorized school, district and state level users access to information and reports meant to support improvements in teaching, learning and educational outcomes. It also integrates longitudinal data from early education through post-secondary education and the workforce. Please note that access to Edwin Analytics is restricted, since some reports contain sensitive student-level data. Visit the Edwin FAQ page for more information.

Description:
The EEC Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Dashboard provides data on key metrics concerning early education and care across the Commonwealth.

This dashboard is used to track the progress of metrics aligned to EEC's strategic objectives and priorities and also increase transparency and public access to KPIs and other data metrics of importance.

Description: Assess and compare how schools and districts allocate resources and serve students with disabilities. This dashboard provides district and comparison data on special education enrollment, staffing, and placement trajectories over several years.

Name: Elementary and Secondary Educators by Age Group
Description: This dataset includes the full-time equivalent (FTE) counts and percentages of educators by age group in Massachusetts public schools since 2008. Blank values indicate that data were not reported, unless otherwise noted. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Educators by Age Groups Report

Name: Elementary and Secondary School and District Accountability Classifications
Description: This dataset is a summary of accountability data for public schools and districts from 2023 onward. It includes the overall classification, reason for classification, annual criterion-referenced target percentages and weights, accountability percentile, and cumulative criterion-referenced target percentage. All Massachusetts public schools and districts with sufficient data are classified into one of two categories: schools and districts that require assistance or intervention, and schools and districts that do not require assistance or intervention. This dataset provides information on each district and school's performance against improvement targets and their overall performance compared to other schools in the state. Accountability percentiles are only calculated for schools, and are not available at the district level. For more information about school and district accountability classifications and the appropriate use of accountability data, please see the Accountability Lists, Materials, and Tools page on the District and School Accountability website. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Accountability Report

Name: Elementary and Secondary Staff Attendance
Description:
This dataset contains staff attendance data from 2025 onward, as reported by districts at the end of each school year. In this dataset, an absence is defined as missing more than half of the workday for time that falls outside of paid vacation time or district-approved professional activities. All employees reported on the staff roster are included in the All Staff category, not just Administrators and Teachers. Staff may appear in multiple categories. Please see the Staff Attendance FAQ for more information. In instances where a cell is blank, data is suppressed due to one of the following reasons:
  • The number of staff in a category was fewer than 6
  • Either an Administrators or Teachers count was 1 and the All Staff category equaled the Administrators and Teachers count. In these instances, only the All Staff category is displayed.
This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Staff Attendance Report

Name: Elementary and Secondary Teacher Data
Description:
This dataset contains data about Massachusetts public school teachers and their qualifications by subject since 2008, including the count of teacher full time equivalents (FTE), the ratio of students to teachers, teacher experience, and credentials. Under Massachusetts state law, Commonwealth charter schools have different educator qualification requirements than traditional public schools and generally, as a matter of state law, are not required to be licensed. See the Educator Qualifications in Commonwealth Charter Schools technical advisory for more information. In 2025 and earlier, unusually high student/teacher ratios were sometimes calculated for teacher FTEs between 0.0 and 0.1. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Teacher Data Report List of Subjects

Name: Employment and Earnings of High School Graduates
Description: This dashboard displays longitudinal wage outcomes of Massachusetts students by district, including College and Career Outcomes, Average Annual Earnings by Student Group, and Average Annual Earnings by Industry. Wage records are obtained from the Massachusetts Department of Unemployment Assistance (DUA) using a secure, anonymized matching process with limitations. For details on the process and suppression rules, please refer to the Employment and Earnings of High School Graduates dashboard itself. The data in the dashboard is also published across three datasets in the E2C Hub: Average Earnings by Student Group Average Earnings by Industry College and Career Outcomes

Name: Enrollment: Grade, Race/Ethnicity, Gender, and Selected Populations
Description:
This dataset contains student enrollment data for all Massachusetts public schools and districts since 1994. It is a wide file with three groups of columns representing the following enrollment indicators:
  • Number of students by grade, including pre-kindergarten (PK), kindergarten (K) and special education beyond 12th grade (SP)
  • Percent of students by race and gender
  • Number and percent of students in student groups: First Language Not English, English Learners, Students with Disabilities, High Needs, Low Income, and Economically Disadvantaged
Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Enrollment by Grade Enrollment by Race/Gender Enrollment by Selected Population

Name: Experienced and In-Field Teachers by Race/Ethnicity
Description:
This dataset displays state and district teacher indicators (In-Field or Experienced) by race and ethnicity with rows for different years. It was created as part of a dashboard supporting the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's commitment to provide all students with a racially diverse and culturally responsive educator workforce.
Selected Teacher Indicators: This displays the number and percent of teachers who are licensed in the subject area(s) they teach (In-Field), as well as the number and percent of teachers who have been teaching in a Massachusetts public school for at least three years (Experienced). Note: The In-Field report uses end-of-year EPIMS; the most recently available data are displayed.
This dataset contains the same data that is also published in the Teacher Indicators table on our Educator Dashboard.

Name: FAFSA Completion Dashboard
Description: This dashboard provides information about Massachusetts students completing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) by state, district, school, and student group. The completion percentage in the dashboard is based on Grade 12 enrollment. FAFSA information is from data provided by the federal government to the Department of Higher Education and matched with student level data from the Department of Elementary and Secondary Education. Data is updated in the dashboard twice a month.

Name: Grade Nine Course Passing Report
Description: This dataset shows the number and rate of grade nine students who completed and passed all courses by subject area since 2018. Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Grade Nine Course Passing Report

Name: High School Graduates Attending Higher Education
Description: This dataset provides information about Massachusetts public high school graduates enrolling into institutions of higher education by student group since 2004. It includes the count and percentage of students enrolled in any college or university, as well as a breakdown of enrollment in private vs. public, two-year vs. four-year, and Massachusetts vs. out-of-state institutions. It also includes the percentage of students enrolled in a Massachusetts community college, a Massachusetts state university, or the University of Massachusetts system. The data provided in the report are based on point-in-time matching of graduates with higher education enrollment data from the National Student Clearinghouse (NSC). For more information about working with NSC data, including coverage and FERPA implications, please visit their Working with Our Data page. Results are not reported for higher education enrollments of fewer than 15. Prior to the 2015 high school graduating class, the data refers to students attending college within 16 months of graduating high school. From 2015 on, the data is also provided by high school graduates attending college by the March following their high school graduation year. The percentages in the report are available by college attendee or high school graduate. Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Graduates Attending Higher Ed

Description:
The Department of Economic Research Data Index provides information about the economy and labor market in Massachusetts. It includes links to data tools, analytic reports, data visualizations, dashboards and other resources in the following areas:

Name: Legacy MCAS Achievement Results
Description:
This dataset contains results from the legacy MCAS assessment administered between 2007-2022, including achievement level, participation rate, average scaled score, and median student growth percentile. Data is available for the following years and subjects:
  • Grades 3-8 ELA and math: 2007-2016
  • Grade 10 ELA and math: 2007-2018
  • Grades 5 and 8 STE: 2007-2018
  • High school science: 2007-2022
The Massachusetts Comprehensive Assessment System (MCAS) is a statewide assessment administered to help parents, students, educators, and policymakers determine where districts, schools, and students are meeting expectations and where they need additional support. For more information about MCAS data, please visit the Profiles Help - About the Data page. Current (“Next Gen”) MCAS results can be found in the MCAS Achievement Results dataset. PARCC results can be found on the Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's Profiles site. Results from different assessment systems measure different standards and cannot be directly compared. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide linked as featured content below for more information. Student growth percentile (MEDIAN_SGP) was calculated as a median prior to 2018. Since 2018, it has been calculated as a mean. In 2021, following the COVID-19 pandemic, a baseline SGP method was used to compare growth from 2019 to 2021. For all other years, a cohort referenced model has been used. For more information on SGP calculations, please see the Student Growth page on DESE's website. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Legacy MCAS Achievement Results

Name: Legacy MCAS Open Response Item Results
Description:
This dataset contains results from the open response items administered as part of the legacy MCAS assessment between 2009-2021. Data is only available for students averaging 2 or above on the open response items in the following years and subjects:
  • Grades 3-8 ELA and math: 2007-2016
  • Grade 10 ELA and math: 2007-2018
  • Grades 5 and 8 STE: 2007-2018
  • High school science: 2007-2021
The Massachusetts Comprehensive Assessment System (MCAS) is a statewide assessment administered to help parents, students, educators, and policymakers determine where districts, schools, and students are meeting expectations and where they need additional support. For more information about MCAS data, please visit the Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's Profiles Help - About the Data page. For overall MCAS results from this assessment, visit the Legacy MCAS Achievement Results dataset. Current (“Next Gen”) MCAS results can be found in the MCAS Achievement Results dataset. PARCC results can be found on the Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's Profiles site. Results from different assessment systems measure different standards and cannot be directly compared. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide linked as featured content below for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Legacy MCAS Open Response Item Results

Name: Licensed and Funded Early Education and Care Program Openings and Closings
Description: This dataset includes the number of Department of Early Education and Care (EEC) licensed and funded early education and care programs that have opened or closed historically, along with their capacity. The data are pulled from EEC's Licensing Education Analytic Database (LEAD). Data are updated at the beginning of each month. For a list of current licensed and funded early education and care programs, please visit the Current Licensed and Funded Early Education and Care Programs dataset. This dataset includes: Licensed Programs are programs that are subject to licensing regulations by EEC, which include meeting health and safety standards, training and professional development requirements, criminal and background checks, and maintaining appropriate ratios of educators to children. Licensed programs can include any of the following: family child care and center based (also called “group and school age”) programs Click here for more information on licensed programs. Funded Programs are programs that are not subject to licensure by EEC but have the approval from EEC to provide subsidized child care for low-income or at-risk families. Funded programs can include any of the following: public schools; private schools; religious schools; and government, tribal, and military child care programs. Click here for more information on funded programs. This dataset is part of EEC's Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Dashboard.

Name: Licensed and Funded Early Education and Care Programs
Description:
This dataset includes monthly historic snapshots of programs licensed or funded by the Department of Early Education and Care (EEC). The data is pulled from EEC's Licensing Education Analytic Database (LEAD). For a list of current early education and care programs please visit the Current Licensed and Funded Early Education and Care Programs dataset. Data are updated monthly at the beginning of each month.

Licensed programs are programs that are subject to licensing regulations by EEC, which include meeting health and safety standards, training and professional development requirements, criminal and background checks, and maintaining appropriate ratios of educators to children. Licensed programs can include any of the following: family child care and group and school age programs. For more information on licensed programs click here.

Funded programs are programs that are not subject to licensure by EEC but have the approval from EEC to provide subsidized child care for low-income or at-risk families. Funded programs can include any of the following: public schools; private schools; religious schools; and government, tribal, and military child care programs. For more information on funded providers click here.

The types of programs in this dataset include:

  • Licensed Family Child Care is child care provided in someone's home where the caretaker is not related to the children.

  • Licensed Center-Based Care, sometimes called group and school age programs, provide early education and care for young children in a non-residential setting such as a school or community center. These programs include several types of early education and care such as: Center-based care for infants, toddlers, preschool, or kindergarten age children; Early Head Start and Head Start programs; and Out-of- school time programs that operate before/after school or during the summer.

  • Funded Public Schools that participate in CCFA are included here and public schools that do not participate in CCFA are not included here. For data on all public schools with pre-K enrollment, please see the Pre-Kindergarten and Kindergarten Enrollment dataset.

  • Funded Private Schools that participate in CCFA are included here and private schools that do not participate in CCFA are not included here. For data on all private schools with pre-K enrollment, please see the Non-Public School Enrollment dataset.

Name: Licensed Child Care Search
Description: A geographically-searchable online directory of licensed child care programs across the Massachusetts, published by the Department of Early Education and Care (EEC). The directory can be searched and filtered by program/provider name, program type, age group, specific address, radius from an address, or on a route between two points.

Name: Licensed Residential and Placement (R&P) Programs
Description: The Department of Early Education and Care (EEC) licenses certain Residential Programs, Foster Care Placement Agencies, and Adoption Agencies in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. This dataset provides a count of the number of residential programs, foster care placement agencies, and adoption agencies licensed by EEC, along with their capacity. For more information about residential programs and placement agencies, see EEC's webpage about residential and placement programs. This dataset is part of EEC's Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Dashboard.

Name: Massachusetts Educator License Mapping Tool: Dashboard
Description:
The Massachusetts Educator License Mapping Tool links educator licenses to courses. The current version of the mapping tool reflects several important changes from the 2018-19 pilot version, including:
  • Educators with a moderate disabilities and/or severe disabilities license are considered "in-field" for all courses within the grade span and content areas for the license. (e.g. a teacher with a PreK-8 Moderate Disabilities license will be "in-field" when teaching a grade 5 math course but is considered "out-of-field" if teaching grade 4 music).
  • Legacy licenses are appropriately reflected in the crosswalk.
  • Some specific courses were updated with additional licensure fields deemed appropriate. (e.g. a teacher with a Business license will be "in-field" when teaching a marketing course).
  • Updates to the courses that an Early Childhood or Elementary teacher is "in-field" for teaching (e.g. an Elementary teacher is not "in-field" when teaching Music).
Please note that while the mapping tool can serve as guidance to districts in considering hiring and assignment of individual educators, it does not prohibit a district from assigning an out-of-field educator to a course.

Name: Massachusetts Youth Risk Behavior Survey: Resource Page
Description: The Massachusetts Youth Risk Behavior Survey (MYRBS) was developed by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to monitor priority health risk behaviors that contribute to the leading causes of death, disease, injury, and social problems among youth. The Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education (DESE) — in collaboration with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and the Massachusetts Department of Public Health (DPH) — conducts the Youth Risk Behavior Survey (YRBS) in randomly selected public high schools in every odd-numbered years. The Massachusetts YRBS (MYRBS) focuses on the major risk behaviors that threaten the health and safety of young people. This anonymous survey includes questions about tobacco use, alcohol and other drug use, sexual behaviors that might lead to unintended pregnancy or sexually transmitted disease, dietary behaviors, physical activity, and behaviors associated with intentional or unintentional injuries. Since 2007, the MYRBS has been conducted jointly with the Massachusetts Youth Health Survey (MYHS) developed by DPH.

Name: MassCore Completion
Description: This report provides the number and percentage of Massachusetts public high school graduates who completed the MassCore recommended program of study each year since 2009. MassCore is a state-recommended program of study intended to align high school coursework with college and workforce expectations. The program of studies includes the successful completion of four units of English, four units of mathematics, three units of a lab-based science, three units of history, two units of the same world language, one unit of the arts, and five additional "core" courses. A computer science course that includes rigorous mathematical or scientific concepts and aligns with the 2016 Digital Literacy and Computer Science Framework can substitute for either a mathematics course or a laboratory science course. For more information on MassCore please click here. MassCore completion rates are not reported for any student group where the number graduated is less than 6. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: MassCore

Name: MA Tests for Educator Licensure: MTEL Administrations
Description:
This page consolidates data on past test administrations of the MTEL (Massachusetts Tests for Educator Licensure). Users can view detailed statistics for each cycle of administration, including numbers of test takers and pass rate summaries for each of the subject tests.

The MTEL was initiated by the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education in 1998 to help ensure that Massachusetts educators can communicate adequately with students, parents/guardians, and other educators and that they are knowledgeable in the subject matter of the license(s) sought.

Name: MCAS Achievement Results
Description: This dataset contains MCAS results since 2017, including achievement level, participation rate, average scaled score, and average student growth percentile. The Massachusetts Comprehensive Assessment System (MCAS) is a statewide assessment administered to help parents, students, educators, and policymakers determine where districts, schools, and students are meeting expectations and where they need additional support. A new civics subject test has been administered since 2025. This dataset contains state, district and school results; MCAS results for collaboratives can be found in the Profiles report. Please note that Legacy MCAS results are not included in this dataset, nor are PARCC or other student assessment results. A table documenting the transition from Legacy MCAS over time can be found in the DESE Researcher's Guide linked as featured content below. For more information about MCAS data, click here. Student growth percentile (AVG_SGP) was calculated as a median for 2017. In 2018 and onward, it is a mean. In 2021, a baseline SGP method was used to compare growth from 2019 to 2021, following the COVID-19 pandemic. For all other years, a cohort referenced model is used. For more information on SGP calculations, please see the Student Growth page on DESE's website. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: MCAS Achievement Results

Name: MCAS Alternate Assessment (MCAS-Alt) Achievement Results
Description: This dataset contains MCAS Alternate Assessment (MCAS-Alt) results by percent of students at each achievement level by grade and subject since 2017. The MCAS-Alt evaluates the achievement of students with significant cognitive disabilities. It is offered in every subject and grade for which a statewide academic assessment is offered. MCAS-Alt results are reported based on alternate academic achievement standards, rather than on the grade-level achievement standards used for reporting standard MCAS testing results. For more information about the MCAS-Alt, see the Background section included in the Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's annual State Summary of Participation and Achievement document. Students with significant cognitive disabilities are required by law to participate in statewide academic assessments and to be counted in the achievement results of all assessed students. Only students who meet the eligibility criteria for a student with the most significant cognitive disabilities may be assigned to take the MCAS-Alt. For more information about the participation of students with disabilities in MCAS, please visit the Student Participation page on DESE's website. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: MCAS-Alt Achievement Results

Name: MTEL (Massachusetts Tests for Educator Licensure) Pass Rates by Race/Ethnicity
Description:
This dataset displays MTEL (Massachusetts Tests for Educator Licensure) data for each test subject by race and ethnicity with rows for different years. It was created as part of a dashboard supporting the Massachusetts Department of Elementary and Secondary Education's commitment to provide all students with a racially diverse and culturally responsive educator workforce.
MTEL Pass Rates: This displays the number of candidates (i.e., test takers) who took the Massachusetts Tests for Educator Licensure and the percent who passed during the program year.
This dataset contains the same data that is also published in the MTEL Pass Rate table on our Educator Dashboard.

Name: NAEP Data Explorer
Description:
The NAEP Data Explorer is a web-based application for accessing National Assessment of Educational Progress (NAEP) data. It provides results by jurisdiction (nation, state, Trial Urban Districts, Puerto Rico, the District of Columbia, and Department of Defense schools) for all main subject areas assessed, including mathematics, reading, writing, and science.

Results are available for the nation and participating states and other jurisdictions since 1990, and for selected urban districts since 2002. The tool can be used to view tables, run statistical tests, and create charts or other materials that can be exported, saved, copied, and shared with others.

Also known as The Nation’s Report Card, NAEP provides important information about student academic achievement and learning experiences in various subjects. NAEP does not provide scores for individual students or schools; instead, it offers results for populations of students (e.g., fourth-graders) and subgroups of those populations (e.g., female students, Hispanic students) as well as results regarding subject-matter achievement, instructional experiences, and school environment. NAEP results are based on school and student samples that are carefully designed to accurately represent student populations of interest.

Name: Pathways/Programs Enrollment by Grade, Race/Ethnicity, Gender, and Selected Populations
Description:
Massachusetts offers a variety of pathways and programs to ensure students graduate college and career ready. These include Career Technical Education (CTE) programs, Early College, and Innovation Pathways. For more information about these pathways and programs, visit the Office of College, Career, and Technical Education website. This dataset contains student enrollment data for all public and charter schools and districts since 2022. It is a wide file with three groups of columns representing the following enrollment indicators:

Name: Performance Measurement Reporting System
Description:
The Performance Measurement Reporting System (PMRS) is designed to fulfill the Massachusetts Board of Higher Education’s statutory obligation to “promote accountability for effective management and stewardship of public funds and to achieve and demonstrate measurable educational outcomes” (MGL, Chapter 15a, Section 7). This report is updated annually each spring and provides a comprehensive examination of the performance of each of Massachusetts’ community colleges and state universities on a set of key indicators focused on:
  • Access & Affordability
  • Student Success & Completion
  • Workforce Alignment
  • Fiscal Stewardship
These indicators are tracked with attention to equity gaps for underserved students wherever possible. The metrics are foundational to assessment and accountability, having been incorporated into annual presidential evaluations, program evaluation, and campus- and system-level strategic planning, including the Strategic Plan for Racial Equity and Strategic Public Higher Education Financing.

Name: Plans of High School Graduates
Description: This dataset contains information regarding the intentions of Massachusetts public high school graduates starting from the year 1995 and onward. Students indicate their plans as of the end of the school year. Only schools and districts that serve 12th grade are included. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Plans of High School Grads

Name: Post Secondary: Employment Outcomes Explorer
Description: The Post-Secondary Employment Outcomes (PSEO) is an experimental data product from the US Census Bureau containing earnings outcomes and employment flows for recent graduates of partner colleges and universities. These statistics are generated by matching university transcript data with a national database of jobs. Only graduates who earn at least the annual equivalent of full-time work at the prevailing federal minimum wage and work three or more quarters in a calendar year are included.

Name: Pre-Kindergarten and Kindergarten Enrollment
Description: This dataset contains pre-kindergarten and kindergarten enrollment data for all Massachusetts public and charter schools and districts since 2018. Both the number and percent of pre-kindergarten and full-day kindergarten students are reported by student group. The number of part-time, tuitioned, and full-time kindergarten students are also reported by the same student groups. Economically Disadvantaged was used 2015-2021. Low Income was used prior to 2015, and a different version of Low Income has been used since 2022. Please see the DESE Researcher's Guide for more information. This dataset contains the same data that is also published on our DESE Profiles site: Enrollment by Pre-Kindergarten Enrollment by Kindergarten

Name: Public Postsecondary Annual Enrollment: Detail
Description: This dataset contains the total annual FTE and unduplicated headcount enrollment for undergraduate and graduate students in credit-bearing courses at public community colleges and state universities in Massachusetts since 2014. Data are disaggregated by fiscal year, segment, institution, and student attributes such as enrollment level, residency, age, race/ethnicity, and gender. This dataset is 1 of 2 datasets that is also published in the interactive Annual Enrollment dashboard on the Department of Higher Education Data Center: 1) Public Postsecondary Annual Enrollment: Detail 2) Public Postsecondary Annual Enrollment: Summary Related datasets: 1) Public Postsecondary Fall Enrollment 2) Public Postsecondary Fall Enrollment by Race and Gender Notes: - Data appear as reported to the Massachusetts Department of Higher Education. - Annual enrollment refers to a 12 month enrollment period over one fiscal year (July 1 through June 30). - Figures published by DHE may differ slightly from figures published by other institutions and organizations due to differences in timing of publication, data definitions, and calculation logic. - Data for the University of Massachusetts are not included due to unique reporting requirements. See Fall Enrollment for HEIRS data on UMass enrollment. -The most common measure of enrollment is headcount of enrolled students. Annual headcount enrollment is unduplicated, meaning any individual student is only counted once per institution and fiscal year, even if they are enrolled in multiple terms. - Enrollment can also be measured as full-time equivalent (FTE) students, a calculation based on the sum of credits carried by all enrolled students. In a fiscal year, 30 undergraduate credits = 1 undergraduate FTE, and 24 graduate credits = 1 graduate FTE at a state university. - For precise calculations and aggregations, use the FTE_RAW column. The FTE column is for display only.

Data are disaggregated by fiscal year, segment, institution, and enrollment level, reporting counts and percentages of total enrollment by gender, race/ethnicity, residency, and age. See Fall Enrollment for HEIRS data on UMass enrollment. -The most common measure of enrollment is headcount of enrolled students. Annual headcount enrollment is unduplicated, meaning any individual student is only counted once per institution and fiscal year, even if they are enrolled in multiple terms. Enrollment can also be measured as full-time equivalent (FTE) students, a calculation based on the sum of credits carried by all enrolled students. In a fiscal year, 30 undergraduate credits = 1 undergraduate FTE, and 24 graduate credits = 1 graduate FTE at a state university.

Data are disaggregated by year, segment, institution, award type, and student attributes such as race and gender. This dataset is 1 of 3 datasets that is also published in the interactive Awards Conferred dashboard on the Department of Higher Education Data Center: 1) Public Postsecondary Awards Conferred by Institution: Detail 2) Public Postsecondary Awards Conferred by Institution: Summary 3) Public Postsecondary Awards Conferred Statewide Notes: - Data appear as reported to the Massachusetts Department of Higher Education. - Figures published by DHE may differ slightly from figures published by other institutions and organizations due to differences in timing of publication, data definitions, and calculation logic. - An award is conferred to a student by a postsecondary educational institution as official recognition after their successful completion of a program of study. - A fiscal year (FY) is 12-month period beginning July 1 and ending June 30. Because a fiscal year straddles two different calendar years, the calendar year and fiscal year will not always match. For example, FY 2025 runs from July 1, 2024 to June 30, 2025.

Name: Public Postsecondary Awards (Degrees) Conferred Statewide
Description: This dataset contains the count of certificates and degrees conferred statewide at all public Massachusetts institutions of higher education since 2014. The data are disaggregated by year, award type, meta-major, and 2 digit CIP Code (Classification of Instructional Program), and student attributes such as race and gender. This dataset is 1 of 3 datasets that is also published in the interactive Awards Conferred dashboard on the Department of Higher Education Data Center: 1) Public Postsecondary Awards Conferred by Institution: Detail 2) Public Postsecondary Awards Conferred by Institution: Summary 3) Public Postsecondary Awards Conferred Statewide This dataset utilizes the 2020 CIP. The Classification of Instructional Programs was created by the U.S. Department of Education's National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) in 1980. More information can be found on the CIP user site. Meta-majors are a framework that categorizes academic programs into broad curricular pathways. A crosswalk of meta-major and CIP is available as a dataset: Awards Conferred Meta-Major to Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) Codes. Notes: - Data appear as reported to the Massachusetts Department of Higher Education. - Figures published by DHE may differ slightly from figures published by other institutions and organizations due to differences in timing of publication, data definitions, and calculation logic. - A fiscal year (FY) is 12-month period beginning July 1 and ending June 30. Because a fiscal year straddles two different calendar years, the calendar year and fiscal year will not always match. For example, FY 2025 runs from July 1, 2024 to June 30, 2025. - An award is conferred to a student by a postsecondary educational institution as official recognition after their successful completion of a program of study.

Name: Public Postsecondary Fall Enrollment: Detail
Description: This dataset contains the total fall enrollment headcount and full-time equivalent (FTE) counts for undergraduate and graduate students at all public Massachusetts institutions of higher education since 2014. Data are disaggregated by the year of fall term, segment, institution, and student attributes such as enrollment level, enrollment status, race/ethnicity, and gender. This dataset is 1 of 2 datasets that is also published in the interactive Fall Enrollment dashboard on the Department of Higher Education Data Center: 1) Public Postsecondary Fall Enrollment: Detail 2) Public Postsecondary Fall Enrollment: Summary Related datasets: 1) Public Postsecondary Annual Enrollment: Detail 2) Public Postsecondary Annual Enrollment: Summary Notes: - Data appear as reported to the Massachusetts Department of Higher Education. - Figures published here may vary from those of individual institutions due to differences in calculation methodologies. - Enrollment can also be measured as full time equivalent (FTE), which is calculated based on the total number of credits carried by enrolled students. In the fall term, 15 undergraduate credits = 1 FTE. For graduate students, 12 credits = 1 FTE at state universities, while at UMass campuses 9 credits = 1 FTE (except for first-professional degree programs which use a 15 credits FTE-standard). - The FTE column is formatted for display. Use the FTE_RAW column to calculate accurate aggregations.

Name: Public Postsecondary First Year Retention by Gender
Description: This dataset contains the total first-year retention rates and counts (Fall to Fall) for new first-time, full-time, degree-seeking undergraduate students, disaggregated by gender. It covers all public community colleges and state universities in Massachusetts, and the University of Massachusetts since 2013. This dataset is 1 of 3 datasets that is also published in the interactive Retention of First-Time, Full-Time Students dashboard on the Department of Higher Education Data Center: Public Postsecondary First Year Retention: Summary Public Postsecondary First Year Retention by Race Public Postsecondary First Year Retention by Gender Definitions: - Retention is characterized when a new first-time, full-time student who was enrolled in their first Fall semester is again enrolled in their second Fall semester. - Adjusted Cohort Count: the number of first-year students in a cohort, excluding those who graduated within the first year. - Retained Any Institution is determined by finding students enrolled in the next Fall at other MA Public Institutions using Massachusetts Department of Higher Education data or elsewhere using NSC data. - Cohort identification: These are the Fall Term Credit Students, identified with a cohort when their current registration = New; their New Student Type = Degree-seeking; and enrollment is full-time with >= 12 credits. Notes: - To comply with data privacy laws, Bucketing is used as a form of data suppression. If a gender category has <= 6 students, then its data is combined with the next largest gender category and the gender is labeled as "Other." - Data appear as reported to the Massachusetts Department of Higher Education.

Name: RADAR: Resource Page
Description: Resource Allocation and District Action Reports (RADAR) enable district leaders to compare their staffing, class size, special education services, school performance, and per-pupil spending data with similar districts. They are intended to support districts in making effective strategic decisions as they develop district plans and budgets. There are three reports: RADAR Benchmarking Online Gives districts an overall picture of how their resources are being allocated and resulting outcomes, including a wide variety of charts and tables. RADAR Special Education Indicates district resources and outcomes related to students with disabilities. This dashboard contains the same data that is also published in the following datasets in the E2C Hub: Special Education Program Characteristics and Student Demographics Special Education Placement Trajectory Students Moving In and Out of Special Education Services Special Education Indicators Special Education Student Progression from High School through Postsecondary Education Student Sending and Receiving Report Shows which towns/cities send students to a particular district, and which districts receive students from a particular town/city. This dashboard contains the same data that is also published in the following datasets in the E2C Hub (dashboard only displays current year of data): Where Residents Go to School (Sending) Reasons for Student Enrollment by Town (Receiving)

Name: RADAR Special Education Dashboard
Description: Assess and compare how schools and districts allocate resources and serve students with disabilities. This dashboard provides district and comparison data on special education enrollment, staffing, and placement trajectories over several years.

Description: This dataset shows the number of students enrolled in a district who are residents of each town since 2014. It includes the reason for enrollment, such as being a resident of the town, school choice, paying tuition, or selecting a charter school. This dataset is one of two containing the same data that is also published in the RADAR Student Sending and Receiving Report (dashboard only displays current year of data): Where Residents Go to School (Sending) Reasons for Student Enrollment by Town (Receiving)

Name: Reducing chronic absenteeism in our schools
Description: This data story illustrates the scope of chronic absenteeism in Massachusetts, how it affects student success, and evidence-based strategies to improve student attendance.

Name: Regional Occupation Alignment Dashboard (ROAD): Resource Page
Description:
This webpage serves as a landing page for the Regional Occupation Alignment Dashboard (ROAD), a tool created and maintained by the Department of Economic Research at the Executive Office of Labor and Workforce Development. It includes an introduction to the dashboard, data sources, methodology, and a user guide. It also provides general information about the Opportunity Star System, the Training Pipeline Alignment, and guidance for how to use the dashboard in combination with local knowledge and regional context.

The Regional Occupation Alignment Dashboard identifies high-demand, high-wage occupations and highlights how local education and training programs align with projected job openings across Massachusetts and its workforce regions. It is designed to help state and regional leaders in workforce development, education, and policy make data-driven decisions about workforce training and education.

Name: SAT Performance
Description: This dataset provides mean SAT scores for all Massachusetts public schools and districts by student group since the 2007 school year.
